content:
About Hartford Foster Futures

We are a small Hartford nonprofit working alongside foster youth, foster families, and the people who support them. Co-founded by Kelly Nelson and Derek Ramirez.
title: About

content:
Contact

Reach the HFF team at hartfordfosterfutures@gmail.com. We are based in Hartford, CT and usually answer within a couple of business days.
title: Contact

content:
Get Involved

Volunteer, mentor, or donate. Whatever time you have, there is a way to put it to use. Start with the volunteer application and we will take it from there.
title: Get Involved

content:
Donate

Every gift goes toward mentorship, resource fairs, and welcome kits for youth entering care. One-time or monthly, whatever works for you.
title: Donate

content:
Lighting the Way Gala

Our annual gala is October 17, 2026 at the Grace Community Church hall. Tickets are $50. Details, sponsorships, and the ticket link live here.
title: Lighting the Way Gala

content:
Our Story

Hartford Foster Futures began in 2024 after Kelly and Derek kept meeting kids who arrived at new homes with nothing but a trash bag. This is how a kitchen-table idea became a working nonprofit.
title: Our Story

content:
Our Team

Meet the people behind HFF: co-founders Kelly Nelson and Derek Ramirez, social lead Sarah Nelson, and volunteer coordinator Adaeze Okonkwo, along with the mentors and volunteers who make the work possible.
title: Our Team

content:
Volunteer

From coat drives to Saturday tutoring, there is a role for every schedule. Fill out the volunteer application and Adaeze will follow up with next steps and the background-check details.
title: Volunteer

content:
Become a Foster Parent

Thinking about fostering in the Hartford area? We walk you through licensing, training, and the support HFF offers once a child is placed with you. You do not have to do it alone.
title: Become a Foster Parent

content:
Mentorship Program

Our mentors commit to a year of steady, low-key support for a young person in care. We handle the matching and training. You bring an hour a week and a willingness to keep showing up.
title: Mentorship Program

content:
Sponsor the Gala

Table and program sponsorships for Lighting the Way come in three levels, each tied to real programs like welcome kits and tutoring. Email Derek to reserve a table for October 17.
title: Sponsor the Gala

content:
Resources

A growing library of guides for foster families, kinship caregivers, and youth aging out: school records checklists, respite options, and local Hartford supports, all in plain language.
title: Resources

content:
Frequently Asked Questions

Do you need to own a home to foster? How long is the background check? What does HFF actually pay for? We answer the questions we hear most, without the jargon.
title: Frequently Asked Questions

content:
Events

Youth group nights, resource fairs, and the annual gala. Here is where to find dates, locations, and sign-up links for everything HFF has coming up around Hartford.
title: Events

content:
Our Impact

Last year HFF matched 24 mentors, packed more than 300 welcome kits, and handed out 400 winter coats. We share our numbers openly so you can see where your support goes.
title: Our Impact

content:
Privacy Policy

How Hartford Foster Futures collects, uses, and protects the information you share through this site and our donation forms. We never sell donor or volunteer data.
title: Privacy Policy

content:
Newsletter

Sign up for the HFF newsletter and get a monthly note from the team: upcoming events, volunteer needs, and a story or two from the Hartford foster community. No spam, ever.
title: Newsletter

content:
Careers

HFF is a lean, mostly volunteer team. When paid roles or paid internships open up, we post them here. Right now we are always looking for mentors and event volunteers.
title: Careers

content:
Partners

We work alongside Hartford churches, schools, and community groups, including Grace Community Church, who host our gala and youth nights. Interested in partnering? We would love to talk.
title: Partners

content:
Ways to Give

Beyond one-time gifts, you can give monthly, sponsor a gala table, run a supply drive, or donate coats and school supplies. Every path helps a Hartford child in care.
title: Ways to Give
